sections.name: AntMatutinumM
lines: While blessed Martin * was offering the sacrament, a ball of fire appeared over his head.;;20
Permit me * to look at heaven, he said, that my spirit may be guided to the Lord and the enemy will find nothing in me and Abraham’s bosom will receive me.;;23
A heavenly * crowd accompanied the body of the blessed man to the place of his burial with harmonious hymns;;97

sections.name: Ant Matutinum 3N
lines: O truly blessed, * O wondrous piety, merciful charity!  He who always longed for the Lord with his inmost being, let him not leave us, let him not ever leave us

sections.name: Responsory4
lines: R. When St Martin was offering the sacrament,
* a ball of fire appeared around his head.
V. For he sent up his prayers to God imploring him for the who flock that had been entrusted to his care.
R. A ball of fire appeared around his head.

lines: @Sancti/11-11:Lectio5:s/At the .*//s

sections.name: Lectio7
lines: @Sancti/11-11:Lectio5:s/.* At the /At the /s

sections.name: Responsory8
lines: R. O wondrous man not overcome by the work nor vanquished by death
* Who neither feared death nor was reluctant to live.
V. O truly blessed in whose mouth there was no deceit, nothing was on his tongue except Christ, except peace, except mercy.
R. Who neither feared death nor was reluctant to live.

lines: @Sancti/11-11:Lectio8:s/Under .*//s

sections.name: Lectio11
lines: @Sancti/11-11:Lectio8:s/.* Under /Under /s s/But .*//s

sections.name: Responsory11
lines: R. Martin, priest of God, who following after the apostles, the Lord united such grace in that he revived three people from death and showed other virtues brilliantly, placing him among the other martyrs and confessors
* And a rose shone in Gaul
V. Indeed though his virgin body he was persuasive in his words, his manner was gracious, his mind stretched forth, raised to heaven
R. And a rose shone in Gaul

lines: @Sancti/11-11:Lectio8:s/.* But /But /s
